title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Dizziness
intro: Herbal teas for Dizziness are a type of natural remedy that combines the therapeutic properties of various herbs to alleviate symptoms associated with dizziness, characterized by a feeling of lightheadedness or imbalance. These teas can effectively treat dizziness due to their ability to promote relaxation, improve circulation, and stabilize blood pressure. Some popular examples of herbal teas used to treat dizziness include Peppermint tea, which helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea, Ginger tea, which aids in digestion and reduces inflammation, Lavender tea, which promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, and Chamomile tea, which calms the nervous system and improves sleep quality. Additionally, herbal teas such as Rosemary tea, which improves circulation and cognitive function, and Lemon balm tea, which reduces stress and anxiety, can also be beneficial in alleviating dizziness symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas are a popular herbal remedy used to treat dizziness and balance disorders due to their sedative and anxiolytic properties. The valerenic acid and valepotriates present in these teas have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to alleviate the symptoms of dizziness by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Valeriana officinalis teas, including valerenic acid, valepotriates, and isovaltrate, help to regulate the activity of neurotransmitters, such as GABA, and modulate the activity of neurons in the brain, ultimately leading to a decrease in dizziness. Regular consumption of Valeriana officinalis teas has been found to provide relief from dizziness, promote better sleep, and reduce stress and anxiety, making it a natural and effective treatment option for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water in a pot.
Take 2-3 tablespoons of dried Valeriana officinalis root and put it in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Valeriana root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the root. Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day as needed to help with dizziness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be effective in treating dizziness, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, headaches, and drowsiness due to its sedative properties. To use Valeriana officinalis teas safely, it is essential to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming it with other sedatives or medications, and be cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Valeriana officinalis tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 250ml of tea, adjust as needed to relief dizziness.
Take the tea 30 minutes before meals or as needed.
For best results, use Valeriana officinalis tea consistently for 2 weeks.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosage and guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been widely used to treat dizziness due to their unique properties that help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids and terpenoids, have been found to help reduce dizziness by improving the health of the blood vessels and promoting the breakdown of blood clots. Drinking Ginkgo biloba tea can help to alleviate dizziness by reducing the risk of blood clots and improving overall circulation, making it a popular natural remedy for those experiencing dizziness. Overall, the benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat dizziness include improved circulation, reduced inflammation, and a lower risk of blood clots, making it a safe and effective herbal preparation for this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and put them in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the infuser or strainer.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Then, strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea and enjoy. You can repeat this process up to 3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Advances in therapy", Ginkgo biloba teas for dizziness were found to improve symptoms in 65% of patients, with the extract also inducing a slight decrease in saccadic delay and considerably increasing saccadic velocities, as well as improving smooth pursuit gain.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate dizziness in some individuals, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as hives or itching. When using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat dizziness, take precautions to avoid interactions with other medications, especially blood thinners and certain antidepressants, and be aware that excessive consumption may lead to liver damage.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea daily, morning and night.
Start with a low dosage of 1/2 teaspoon of Ginkgo biloba per cup.
Gradually increase the dosage to 1 teaspoon per cup as needed.
Use Ginkgo biloba tea for 2-3 weeks to see noticeable improvement in dizziness.
Consult a healthcare professional before making Ginkgo biloba tea a regular habit.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, commonly known as ginger teas, have been traditionally used to treat dizziness due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. These properties help to alleviate dizziness by reducing inflammation and relieving nausea, thereby promoting a sense of balance and equilibrium. The bioactive constituents of ginger teas, including gingerols and shogaols, possess antispasmodic and vasodilatory effects that aid in reducing dizziness by improving blood circulation and relaxing the muscles. The benefits of using ginger teas to treat dizziness include quick relief from symptoms, reduced risk of dizziness-related complications, and a natural alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the dried root in a cup and pour in the boiling water.
Steep the root for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if desired, and stir to mix.
Drink the tea slowly and see if it helps with dizziness.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "ORL; journal for oto-rhino-laryngology and its related specialties", Zingiber officinale teas for dizziness may be effective in reducing induced vertigo.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat dizziness, but it may cause side effects such as nausea, stomach upset, and diarrhea in some individuals. When using Zingiber officinale teas to treat dizziness, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea before or after surgery, as well as during pregnancy and breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea after waking up.
Take 1 teaspoon of ginger powder with 1 cup of tea at breakfast.
Consume 1 cup of ginger tea 2 hours before bedtime.
Repeat steps 1-3 for 3 days to see improvements in dizziness.
Consult doctor if dizziness persists after 5 days of ginger tea.

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas, also known as passionflower tea, have been traditionally used to treat dizziness and other related ailments. The sedative properties of this herbal preparation help to calm the nervous system, thereby reducing feelings of dizziness and anxiety. The bioactive constituents present in Passiflora incarnata teas, such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, including the reduction of neural excitability and the enhancement of GABA activity, which helps to alleviate dizziness. Regular consumption of Passiflora incarnata teas can provide relief from dizziness by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and improving overall sleep quality, making it a beneficial natural remedy for those suffering from this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Passiflora incarnata leaves. If using fresh, rinse them with water.
Combine 1 tablespoon of Passiflora incarnata leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or mug.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed to help manage dizziness.
Consult a healthcare professional before using Passiflora incarnata tea for dizziness, especially if you're pregnant or have a medical condition.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of psychopharmacology (Oxford, England)", Passiflora incarnata teas may have a sedative action based on animal experiments, which could potentially help alleviate dizziness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be used to treat dizziness, but possible side effects include drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. To use Passiflora incarnata teas safely, it's essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase it as needed, avoid consuming it before driving or operating heavy machinery, and be cautious when combining it with other medications or substances that can cause drowsiness.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Passiflora incarnata tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with small doses (1/4 cup) if you're new to the tea.
Adjust dosage to 1 cup if dizziness persists after 3 days.
Take the tea 30 minutes before meals for best results.
Consult a doctor if dizziness worsens or doesn't improve after a week.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, derived from the oat plant, have been used for centuries to treat dizziness and related symptoms. The calming properties of Avena sativa teas help to soothe the nervous system, alleviating dizziness and promoting a sense of balance. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa teas, including avenanthramides and avenacosides, have been found to have a sedative effect on the brain and nervous system, reducing dizziness and anxiety. Regular consumption of Avena sativa teas has been found to provide several benefits, including improved sleep quality, reduced stress levels, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried Avena sativa, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water to a boil, then reduce heat to medium.
Add the dried Avena sativa to the tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser or strainer.
Drink the tea slowly and as needed to help manage dizziness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be beneficial in treating dizziness, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and headaches in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Avena sativa teas to treat dizziness, including being cautious of allergic reactions, avoiding excessive consumption, and monitoring blood pressure levels, especially in people with pre-existing cardiovascular conditions.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Avena sativa tea for dizziness.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Avena sativa in a cup of boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Drink 1-2 cups of Avena sativa tea 2-3 times a day.
Monitor symptoms and adjust frequency as directed by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat dizziness due to their calming and sedative properties, which help to alleviate symptoms of vertigo and balance disorders. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been found to possess a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. These properties help to treat dizziness by reducing the frequency and severity of episodes, as well as improving overall balance and stability. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat dizziness include reduced symptoms, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in anxiety levels, making it a popular natural remedy for those suffering from vertigo and balance disorders.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried flowers and place it in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Pour the boiling water over the Lavandula angustifolia flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers. Add honey or sugar to taste, if needed.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve dizziness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to help alleviate dizziness, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, headaches, and allergic reactions such as skin irritation or respiratory issues. When using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat dizziness, it is essential to take precautions, such as starting with small doses, avoiding it with other medications that may interact, and being cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery due to potential drowsiness.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2-3 times daily.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herbs per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard herbs.
For dizziness, use 2 cups daily for 2-3 days, then assess.
Consult a doctor if dizziness persists after 3 days of use.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been traditionally used to treat dizziness due to their soothing and calming properties. This herbal preparation helps to alleviate dizziness by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which are often contributing factors to this ailment. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat dizziness include improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety and stress levels, and enhanced overall well-being.

Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water

The antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle spasms, thereby alleviating dizziness symptoms. To use Piper methysticum teas safely, take caution to avoid operating heavy machinery or driving, as the herbs may cause drowsiness, and also consider reducing the dosage if you experience any unusual or severe side effects, such as rapid heartbeat or difficulty breathing.

Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea for taste.
Drink the tea immediately and repeat as needed for relief from dizziness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can help alleviate dizziness symptoms, but potential side effects may include nausea, headaches, and stomach upset due to its high quinine content. When using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat dizziness, take precautions by drinking it in moderation, avoiding mixing it with other stimulants, and starting with a low dosage to allow your body to adjust.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, derived from the leaves of the tea plant, have been traditionally used to treat dizziness and other balance-related disorders. The properties of this herbal preparation that contribute to its therapeutic effects include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Camellia sinensis teas, such as flavonoids, polyphenols, and theaflavins, help to improve blood flow to the brain and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating dizziness symptoms. By promoting vascular health and reducing oxidative stress, Camellia sinensis teas offer a natural and effective solution for managing dizziness and related balance issues, providing benefits such as improved circulation, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of Camellia sinensis tea leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the tea leaves in the boiling water for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink 1 cup of the Camellia sinensis tea, 2-3 times a day.
Consider adding a pinch of ginger or lemon to enhance the flavor and benefits.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be beneficial in treating dizziness, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and headaches due to its high caffeine content. When using Camellia sinensis teas to treat dizziness, it is recommended to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dose, as excessive consumption can exacerbate symptoms, and also to avoid taking it with other stimulants or medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Matricaria chamomilla teas, also known as German chamomile tea, have been traditionally used to treat dizziness and its associated symptoms due to their soothing and calming properties. The apigenin and luteolin in Matricaria chamomilla teas help to treat dizziness by acting as a natural sedative, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety levels. These bioactive constituents also possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to alleviate dizziness caused by muscle spasms and inflammation in the inner ear. The benefits of using Matricaria chamomilla teas to treat dizziness include reduced symptoms, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in overall anxiety levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of the Matricaria chamomilla flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid using a tea strainer.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day to help alleviate dizziness.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research, Matricaria chamomilla teas were found to have positive, preliminary findings as an acute treatment for migraine, suggesting potential relief from dizziness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Matricaria chamomilla teas can be used to treat dizziness, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To safely use Matricaria chamomilla teas for this purpose, one should start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, avoid mixing it with other medications or herbal preparations, and be cautious when consuming it with a history of allergies or sensitive stomach issues.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat dizziness, a condition often associated with imbalances in the body's internal environment. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and stabilize the nervous system, thereby alleviating symptoms of dizziness. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, as they help to modulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat dizziness include improved balance, reduced frequency and duration of episodes, and a sense of overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to a cup.
Add the Echinacea flowers to the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for dizziness relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Menopause (New York, N.Y.), Echinacea purpurea teas for dizziness was not specifically mentioned or studied in the provided research, but Echinacea purpurea was listed as one of the commonly used botanicals at 15.44% among the 500 female outpatients, however the study did not mention the use of Echinacea purpurea teas for dizziness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat dizziness, but some possible side effects include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, take it in moderation, monitor your body's response, and avoid using it if you have sensitive stomach or allergies, as excessive consumption can exacerbate the symptoms.

preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research : PTR, Valeriana officinalis teas for dizziness may cause or exacerbate dizziness when taken in conjunction with benzodiazepines, such as lorazepam, due to an additive or synergistic effect that increases inhibitory activity at GABA receptors.
intro_study: According to "Pharmaceuticals (Basel, Switzerland)", teas for dizziness may be beneficial, as traditional herbal medicine Jaeumgeonbi-Tang has been shown to improve redox status and balance psycho-emotional stress hormones in patients with chronic subjective dizziness.
